name: social_services_classroom_monitoring_oakline_jus_mer
premise: Stakeholders disagree on what counts as verified fact versus prudent assumption. Stakeholders disagree on what counts as verified fact versus prudent assumption. The situation maps to internal runbooks and external messaging tied to: social services classroom monitoring oakline jus mer. The moral tension is explicitly framed as justice versus mercy: leadership cannot fully satisfy both without accepting tradeoffs. Two upstream teams have published contradictory status pages; customers are comparing timestamps. Legal and comms are on hold; you must record a decision in the incident audit trail within the next fifteen minutes. Anyone affected by an automated hold or model output must retain a human escalation path that is actually staffed. Any exception must be time-boxed, named, and reversible where technically feasible.

agents.name: Zara Berg
agents.identity: I am Zara Berg, the accountable decision-maker on this Social Services bridge; I own what gets logged, communicated, and executed in the next window.
agents.goal: I need to stop the bleeding without baking in a rule that later punishes mercy whenever someone invokes justice.

agents.name: Fatima Ishikawa
agents.identity: I am Fatima Ishikawa, responsible for surfacing who gets protected last when systems fail; I am watching for silent harm and unfair distribution.
agents.goal: I want safeguards so mercy is not sacrificed by default just because justice feels morally loud in the moment.

choices: I will choose a narrow, time-boxed authorization with explicit human override, subgroup monitoring, and a hard stop if harm indicators rise—accepting slower relief to protect mercy.

I will choose a broad immediate authorization to stabilize outcomes fast, with transparent metrics and rapid correction afterward—accepting higher risk to mercy to secure justice before the window closes.
forced_choice.call_to_action: External parties will infer a decision if none is posted. With partial evidence, Zara Berg must decide under justice versus mercy. What does Zara Berg do?
